title: Osiris Eyeglasses: Brand Overview, Styles, and Where to Find Them
description: Everything about Osiris eyeglasses — who makes them, popular styles, pricing, and quality. Plus alternatives from similar mid-range optical brands available in Canada.
tldr: Osiris is a mid-range eyewear brand sold primarily through Specsavers optical stores. They offer contemporary men's and women's frames in the $100–$250 range. If Specsavers isn't accessible to you, similar quality and style can be found from brands like Calvin Klein, Nine West, and Polo Ralph Lauren at independent opticians.

definition.text: Osiris is a mid-range eyewear brand offering contemporary prescription glasses and sunglasses, primarily distributed through Specsavers optical retail stores. The brand focuses on modern, wearable styles for men and women at accessible price points.

sections.content:
Osiris is a house brand manufactured for and sold through Specsavers, one of the world's largest optical retail chains. Specsavers operates in the UK, Australia, New Zealand, and several European countries. In Canada, Specsavers has locations primarily in Western provinces.

House brands (also called store brands or private labels) are common in the optical industry. They allow retailers to offer stylish frames at lower prices because there's no designer licensing fee built into the cost. The frames are typically manufactured by the same factories that produce branded eyewear, just without the logo markup.

This is one of the most common questions that comes up during eye exams, and the answer often depends on individual factors that only a professional assessment can evaluate. What works for one person may not be ideal for another — your prescription, daily activities, facial anatomy, and budget all play a role in determining the best approach.

If you're in the Edmonton area, Charm Optical offers comprehensive eye exams that go beyond just measuring your prescription. Their optometrists assess your overall eye health, discuss your visual needs, and recommend lens and frame options tailored to your lifestyle. With direct insurance billing for most Alberta providers and same-day glasses for many prescriptions, getting the right eyewear doesn't have to be complicated or expensive. Book online — appointments are usually available within a few days.

Osiris positions itself as a contemporary, accessible brand with clean designs. Their collections typically include:

  • Men's: Rectangle, square, and round frames in metal and acetate. Neutral colours (black, tortoiseshell, gunmetal, navy) dominate. The styling leans professional and understated.
  • Women's: Cat-eye, oval, and rectangle shapes with more colour variety including burgundy, clear, and two-tone options. Some styles incorporate subtle pattern or texture details.

Build quality is typical of the mid-range tier — comparable to other house brands like Specsavers' Kylie Minogue or Jeff Banks lines, and roughly equivalent to mid-range retail brands. Hinges are standard (not spring-loaded on most models), and materials are primarily standard acetate and stainless steel.

The best way to evaluate frame styles is to try them on in person. Photos and online try-on tools give a rough idea, but they can't replicate how a frame feels on your face — the weight distribution, the temple pressure, and how the nose bridge sits. These comfort factors matter more than aesthetics for glasses you'll wear 12+ hours a day.

Charm Optical in Edmonton carries over 500 frames from brands including Ray-Ban, Oakley, Versace, Tom Ford, Burberry, Prada, and Dolce & Gabbana. Their opticians help you narrow down the options based on your face shape, prescription requirements (especially important for progressives), and personal style preferences. With same-day service for many prescriptions, you can walk in and walk out with new glasses the same day.

Osiris frames typically fall in the $100–$250 CAD range depending on the specific model and any bundle deals Specsavers offers. Many Specsavers locations run '2 for 1' or 'frames from $X' promotions that include Osiris models.

At this price point, you're getting functional, stylish frames without the premium of designer names. The lenses (which are the functional part of your glasses) can be upgraded to the same quality tiers regardless of frame brand.

sections.h2: Alternatives to Osiris at Similar Price Points
sections.content:
If you're interested in Osiris-style eyewear but don't have a Specsavers nearby (or want to shop at an independent optician), these brands offer comparable style and quality:

BrandStylePrice RangeAvailable At
Calvin KleinClean, modern, minimal$150–$280Charm Optical
Nine WestContemporary women's styles$120–$220Charm Optical
Polo Ralph LaurenClassic, preppy$180–$300Charm Optical
BurberrySophisticated, British heritage$250–$400Charm Optical
Ray-BanIconic, versatile$200–$350Charm Optical

The advantage of shopping at an independent optical store is personalized fitting, direct insurance billing, and access to multiple brands rather than being limited to one retailer's house labels. Charm Optical direct-bills most Alberta insurance providers, which often makes designer frames more affordable than expected.

Pricing varies significantly depending on where you buy and what lens features you choose. In Canada, the total cost of a complete pair of glasses (frame + lenses) typically breaks down as follows: budget options start at $150-$250 for basic frames with standard single vision lenses. Mid-range options with anti-reflective coating and a name-brand frame run $300-$500. Premium designer frames with progressive or high-index lenses can reach $600-$900+. The biggest variable is usually the lens — a basic single vision lens costs far less than a digital freeform progressive with premium coatings.

Insurance makes a significant difference. Most employer health plans in Alberta cover $200-$500 toward glasses every 1-2 years. Charm Optical direct-bills most Alberta insurance providers — Alberta Blue Cross, Canada Life, Desjardins, and more — so you only pay the difference at the counter. No paperwork, no reimbursement wait. Book an eye exam to get your prescription checked and learn exactly what your plan covers before you shop.

sections.h2: House Brand vs. Designer Frames: What's the Real Difference?
sections.content:
The honest answer: sometimes very little, sometimes a lot.

  • Construction: Premium designer frames (Ray-Ban, Persol, Oakley) generally use better hinges (spring hinges are more common), thicker acetate, and more refined finishing. Entry-level designer frames can be similar to good house brands.
  • Materials: High-end brands more frequently use premium materials like titanium, Italian Mazzucchelli acetate, and stainless steel. House brands typically use standard acetate and base metals.
  • Design: Designer brands invest heavily in frame engineering and styling. House brands often follow trends set by designers, delivering a similar look at a lower price.
  • Longevity: Better materials and hinges mean designer frames typically hold up longer with daily wear. A Ray-Ban that lasts 3–4 years before needing replacement versus a house brand that lasts 1–2 years changes the value equation.
  • Warranty: Designer brands often come with manufacturer warranties against defects. House brand warranties vary by retailer.

sections.h2: How to Choose the Right Lenses
sections.content:
Choosing the right lenses starts with understanding your prescription and daily visual demands. Your optometrist will assess your refraction, measure your pupillary distance, and discuss your lifestyle needs — whether you spend most of your day on screens, driving, reading, or working outdoors. Each of these activities benefits from specific lens designs and coatings.

Lens material matters too. Standard CR-39 plastic is affordable and optically clear, but higher prescriptions benefit from high-index materials (1.60, 1.67, or 1.74) that produce thinner, lighter lenses. Polycarbonate and Trivex offer impact resistance for active lifestyles and children's glasses. The right combination of material, design, and coatings gives you the clearest, most comfortable vision possible.

The best way to navigate these choices is with professional guidance. A comprehensive eye exam at Charm Optical includes a detailed discussion of lens options tailored to your specific needs. The opticians at Charm Optical in Edmonton can walk you through each option side by side, and many lens types are available for same-day glasses at Charm Optical, so you don't have to wait days for your new glasses.

Your eyes are one of the most important tools you have, and they deserve proper care and correction. The technology behind modern eyewear has advanced remarkably in recent years — from digital freeform progressive lenses that customize the optics to your exact wearing position, to lightweight titanium frames that weigh less than a coin, to anti-reflective coatings that virtually eliminate glare. Understanding these options empowers you to make better choices about your eye health and get glasses that genuinely improve your quality of life rather than just meeting a basic need.

Regular eye exams are the foundation of good vision care. The Canadian Association of Optometrists recommends exams every one to two years for adults and annually for children, seniors over 65, and anyone with diabetes or a family history of eye disease. A comprehensive exam does far more than measure your prescription — your optometrist checks the health of your retina, optic nerve, and blood vessels, catching conditions like glaucoma and macular degeneration in their earliest stages. Many serious eye conditions have no noticeable symptoms until significant damage has occurred, making preventive exams essential.

The best next step is always a professional eye exam. Your optometrist can evaluate your specific needs and recommend the right combination of lenses, coatings, and frames for your lifestyle and budget. In Alberta, most employer insurance plans cover comprehensive eye exams, and children under 18 and adults over 65 receive coverage through Alberta Health.

Charm Optical at 5035 Ellerslie Rd SW in South Edmonton offers thorough eye exams with experienced optometrists, a selection of over 500 designer frames from brands like Ray-Ban, Oakley, and Versace, and same-day glasses for many prescription types. They offer direct insurance billing for most Alberta providers, so you only pay the difference at the counter.

Whether you are buying your first pair of glasses, upgrading to progressive lenses, or just need an adjustment on your current frames, having a trusted local optician makes the process smoother. Book an appointment online — most appointments are available within a few days, and walk-ins are welcome for adjustments and frame browsing.

faqs.q: Is Osiris a good glasses brand?
faqs.a: Osiris is a solid mid-range brand offering contemporary styles at accessible prices. Quality is typical for the price tier — functional and stylish for everyday wear. For better longevity and construction, consider stepping up to brands like Ray-Ban or Calvin Klein.

faqs.q: Where can I buy Osiris glasses in Canada?
faqs.a: Osiris is exclusive to Specsavers optical stores. If there's no Specsavers in your area, independent optical stores carry comparable brands. In Edmonton, Charm Optical has similar mid-range options from Calvin Klein, Nine West, and Polo Ralph Lauren.

faqs.q: Are Osiris glasses the same quality as designer brands?
faqs.a: They're in the mid-range tier — good quality for daily use but generally below premium designer brands in terms of hinge quality, material grade, and longevity. The styling can be very similar to designer frames at a lower price point.

faqs.q: What are good alternatives to Osiris eyeglasses?
faqs.a: Calvin Klein, Nine West, and Polo Ralph Lauren offer similar contemporary styling with slightly better build quality. All three are available at Charm Optical in Edmonton with professional fitting and insurance direct billing.

faqs.q: What lens material is best for strong prescriptions?
faqs.a: High-index lenses (1.67 or 1.74) are best for strong prescriptions because they bend light more efficiently, resulting in thinner and lighter lenses. Standard plastic (CR-39) works well for mild prescriptions. Your optician at Charm Optical can recommend the ideal index based on your prescription strength.

faqs.q: How often should I replace my lenses?
faqs.a: Most optometrists recommend updating your prescription every 1-2 years. Lens coatings (anti-reflective, scratch-resistant) degrade over time even if your prescription hasn't changed. If you notice scratches, haze, or peeling, it's time for new lenses. Book an eye exam at Charm Optical to check your current prescription.
